title: AD Taylor-tower P0: standalone C proof-of-concept for arbitrary-order univariate Taylor recurrences
goal: Phase-0 of the arbitrary-order AD design (docs/design/AD_TAYLOR_TOWER.md). Self-contained C (no repo deps) implementing the truncated-Taylor recurrence kernel: t_mul (Cauchy convolution), t_div, t_exp, t_log, t_sincos (coupled), t_pow. Seed x = {x0,1,0,...}; compute x^5, sin/cos, exp, 1/(1-x), log(1+x); recover f^(k)=k!*c[k] for k=0..8 and compare to closed-form analytic derivatives. Proves the recurrences before any compiler integration.
acceptance: tests/ad_taylor_poc/run.sh compiles with cc and exits 0
all derivatives k=0..8 match analytic at rel-err < 1e-12 (63/63 checks)
campaign_payoff: De-risks the whole tower design: closed recurrences are exact and polynomial-cost, killing the 2^K hyper-dual wall (ESH-0118) before compiler work starts.
